evidence: ExplorationOptions is used for multi-seed (SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMultiSeed) and comparison (compareCollocateProfiles) — two use cases sharing the same record.

SingleSeedExplorationOptions.toBaseOptions() projects to ExplorationOptions — the Javadoc says 'extends shared ExplorationOptions parameters' indicating SingleSeedExplorationOptions is the specialisation of ExplorationOptions.
A reader searching for 'multi-seed options' will search for 'MultiSeed' and find nothing, then must discover via Javadoc that ExplorationOptions is the multi-seed record.

issues.summary: round2dp in ExploreResponseAssembler uses an abbreviated numeric suffix where the rest of the utility surface uses full descriptive names
issues.suggestion: Rename to roundToTwoDecimalPlaces or roundTwoDecimalPlaces to match the full-word convention. Alternatively, if the codebase accepts numeric shorthand in names, document it in a style note — but 2dp appears nowhere else.
evidence: Method: 'public static double round2dp(double value)' — '2dp' abbreviates '2 decimal places'.

All other static utility methods in the codebase use full words: LogDiceCalculator.compute, LogDiceCalculator.relativeFrequency, CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens, CqlUtils.mergeLemmaConstraint, RelationPatternBuilder.buildFullPattern, RelationUtils.resolveRelationAlias.
round2dp is called 12 times within ExploreResponseAssembler itself — renaming is a contained change.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti uses direct sendError for nouns_per validation instead of the IAE-throw convention used by all sibling handlers
issues.suggestion: Replace the direct sendError with 'throw new IllegalArgumentException("nouns_per is not supported for multi-seed exploration")' and remove the early return. wrapWithErrorHandling will convert it to a 400 response with the message, consistent with all other validation paths in ExplorationHandlers.
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: 'if (params.containsKey("nouns_per")) { HttpApiUtils.sendError(exchange, 400, "nouns_per is not supported..."); return; }' — direct sendError before buildExploreRequest.
Sibling method handleSemanticFieldExplore uses resolveRelationConfig which throws IAE('Unknown relation: ...'), picked up by wrapWithErrorHandling → 400.
handleSemanticFieldComparison throws IAE('Comparison requires at least 2 seed nouns...') — also IAE-throw pattern.
The nouns_per check is a validation failure of the same class as 'missing required param' or 'unknown relation', but uses a different mechanism.

issues.summary: RelationUtils.resolveRelationAlias silently returns null for null input instead of failing fast with IAE, relying on callers to avoid null
issues.suggestion: Change resolveRelationAlias to accept @NonNull String relation (throwing IAE if null) and remove the null-passthrough branch. Update the @return Javadoc. Callers that need null-safe behavior can guard before calling.
evidence: RelationUtils.resolveRelationAlias: '@return the canonical grammar-config ID, or null if relation is null' — returns null by design.

ExplorationHandlers.resolveRelationConfig calls: 'RelationUtils.resolveRelationAlias(params.getOrDefault("relation", "noun_adj_predicates"))' — getOrDefault prevents null in practice, but the method signature makes null a valid input/output.

If a future caller passes null (e.g. from a misconfigured default), the result null propagates to grammarConfig.getRelation(null) which returns Optional.empty(), then resolveRelationConfig throws IAE('Unknown relation: null') — a confusing error message compared to 'relation must not be null'.

issues.summary: round2dp uses abbreviated numeric jargon (2dp = 2 decimal places) instead of a descriptive method name
issues.suggestion: Rename to roundToTwoDecimals or roundTwoDecimalPlaces. The method body is trivial (Math.round(value * 100.0) / 100.0) so the name should carry the full meaning.
evidence: 'public static double round2dp(double value)' — '2dp' is not a standard Java naming convention; it is shorthand from British numeric notation.
Called 12 times within ExploreResponseAssembler; no other method in the codebase uses numeric shorthand in its name.
Every other utility method uses full words: LogDiceCalculator.compute, LogDiceCalculator.relativeFrequency, CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens.

issues.summary: ExploreParams (private record) abbreviates Exploration to Explore in the same class that uses ExplorationOptions as the public equivalent
issues.suggestion: Rename the private record to ExplorationParams or ExplorationParameters to match the Exploration prefix. Since it is a private record, the rename is contained to ExplorationHandlers.
evidence: ExplorationHandlers private record: 'private record ExploreParams(int topCollocates, int minShared, double minLogDice, int nounsPerCollocate)'.
The public equivalent type (passed to SemanticFieldExplorer) is ExplorationOptions — same concept, full word.
Within ExplorationHandlers, resolveExploreParams() returns ExploreParams, then converts to ExplorationOptions — the abbreviated type name directly precedes the full-word type name at the boundary.

issues.summary: getCorsAllowOrigin() is a private getter that just returns a private static final constant, adding a naming layer with no signal
issues.suggestion: Remove getCorsAllowOrigin() and have setCorsHeader reference CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN directly. This eliminates a pointless indirection while keeping the constant's descriptive name visible at each use site.
evidence: 'private static String getCorsAllowOrigin() { return CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN; }' — the getter returns the constant directly with no logic.

CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN is already a well-named static final: 'Cached at class-load time from the cors.allow.origin JVM system property'. Reading the constant name gives the same information as calling the getter.

All four callers within HttpApiUtils (setCorsHeader, which is called by sendJsonResponse, sendError, sendOptionsResponse, sendBinaryResponse) could reference CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN directly without losing clarity.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer is a thin facade that adds no policy, routing, or translation — it delegates entirely to three collaborators
issues.suggestion: Either give SemanticFieldExplorer a genuine coordination responsibility (e.g., enforcing cross-cutting constraints like max seed count, caching, or request tracing) or dissolve it and let api/ call MultiSeedExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator directly. If the facade serves as a future extension point, document that explicitly in its Javadoc to justify the indirection.
evidence: exploreMultiSeed delegates entirely: 'return multiSeedExplorer.computeCollocateIntersection(seeds, relationConfig, opts)'.
compareCollocateProfiles delegates entirely: 'return comparator.compareCollocateProfiles(seedNouns, opts.minLogDice(), opts.topCollocates())'.

exploreByPattern is the only method with meaningful logic (candidate selection, core collocate identification) — all done in a private exploreWithCandidates method. The public exploreByPattern simply validates and delegates to the private method.

ExplorationHandlers could directly call MultiSeedExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator via SemanticFieldExplorer's constructor-injected collaborators — SemanticFieldExplorer adds no observable policy of its own for the multi-seed and comparison paths.

issues.summary: RelationUtils bundles a simple alias map lookup (resolveRelationAlias) with a grammar-config search algorithm (findBestCollocatePattern) that have no shared state or cohesion
issues.suggestion: Move findBestCollocatePattern to RelationPatternBuilder (which already builds patterns from RelationConfig) or inline it in CollocateProfileComparator.deriveAdjectivePattern if it has only one caller. Keep RelationUtils as the alias-resolution utility only.
evidence: resolveRelationAlias is a Map.getOrDefault lookup against a static final map — O(1), no dependencies beyond the alias map.

findBestCollocatePattern takes a GrammarConfig, filters relations by RelationType, delegates to RelationPatternBuilder.buildCollocateReversePattern — a multi-step algorithm that searches the grammar config.

These two methods have no shared data: resolveRelationAlias uses the static RELATION_ALIASES map; findBestCollocatePattern uses the grammarConfig argument. The only shared connection is 'both deal with relations'.

findBestCollocatePattern is only called from CollocateProfileComparator.deriveAdjectivePattern — a better home would be a static method on CollocateProfileComparator itself or a dedicated RelationSelector helper.

issues.summary: buildCollocateReversePattern hardcodes Penn Treebank xpos regex strings in a switch instead of delegating to PosGroup
issues.suggestion: Add a method toCqlConstraint() to PosGroup that returns the xpos regex string for each enum value, or maintain a static Map<PosGroup, String> in RelationPatternBuilder. Either approach consolidates the mapping in one place.
evidence: buildCollocateReversePattern: 'return switch (config.collocatePosGroup()) { case ADJ -> "[xpos=\"JJ.*\"]"; case VERB -> "[xpos=\"VB.*\"]"; case NOUN -> "[xpos=\"NN.*\"]"; case ADV -> "[xpos=\"RB.*\"]" }'.

PosGroup already has the canonical name for each group (ADJ, VERB, NOUN, ADV) — the CQL constraint string is a derivation of the PosGroup that should live on PosGroup or in a single mapping, not distributed across a switch in RelationPatternBuilder.
If a new PosGroup variant is added, both PosGroup and this switch must be updated — split responsibility for the same concept.

issues.summary: BlackLab 4.x resolved from a raw GitHub content URL that has no SLA and can become unavailable, breaking CI reproducibility
issues.suggestion: Run ./install-blacklab.sh to install the JAR to the local Maven repository and remove the raw GitHub repository entry from pom.xml for local development. For CI, mirror the BlackLab artifacts to a stable private registry (Nexus, Artifactory, or GitHub Packages) and update the repository URL. The install script is already present in the repository — this is the documented mitigation that has not been acted on.
evidence: pom.xml repository: '<url>https://raw.githubusercontent.com/instituutnederlandsetaal/BlackLab/mvn-repo/</url>'.

Comment in pom.xml: 'This URL has no SLA and can become unavailable (rate-limited, branch deleted, or repo renamed)'. The risk is acknowledged but the mitigation (local install or private registry) has not been applied.
CI builds that depend on this URL will fail silently if GitHub rate-limits the repo or the branch is renamed — no fallback repository is configured.

issues.summary: API server uses com.sun.net.httpserver (internal JDK API) rather than the public jdk.httpserver module API available since Java 18
issues.suggestion: Add a comment in WordSketchApiServer.java noting that com.sun.net.httpserver is the public API exposed by the jdk.httpserver module since Java 18 (JEP 408) and is stable on Java 21+. If the project ever migrates to a named module, add 'requires jdk.httpserver;' to module-info.java. No immediate code change required for the current unnamed-module setup.
evidence: WordSketchApiServer.java import: 'import com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer' — com.sun.* packages are historically internal JDK APIs.

Java 18 (JEP 408) made the Simple Web Server and associated HttpServer classes part of the standard library via jdk.httpserver module, but the public API uses the same com.sun.net.httpserver package name — it was promoted in-place. However, the project targets Java 21 (maven.compiler.release=21), so the API is accessible as part of the public jdk.httpserver module.

pom.xml does not explicitly require jdk.httpserver in module-info.java or via --add-modules (but no module-info.java exists, so the project is on the unnamed module path, and com.sun.net.httpserver is exported by jdk.httpserver by default in Java 18+).

This is lower risk than originally flagged — Java 21 exports jdk.httpserver publicly. The remaining risk is that the project's pom.xml/build does not explicitly declare the jdk.httpserver dependency, which could create issues in modularized builds.

issues.summary: buildAdjectiveProfileList makes four separate passes over the scores array (average, max, min, variance) instead of a single-pass accumulator
issues.suggestion: Replace the three stream calls and variance loop with a single explicit loop: accumulate sum, sumOfSquares, min, max in one pass; compute avg = sum/n, variance = sumOfSquares/n - avg*avg. This eliminates three array iterations and makes the statistical intent explicit.
evidence: double avgScore = Arrays.stream(scores).average().orElse(0.0);
double maxScore = Arrays.stream(scores).max().orElse(0.0);
double minScore = Arrays.stream(scores).min().orElse(0.0);
Then a separate for loop: 'for (double s : scores) { variance += (s - avgScore) * (s - avgScore); }'
All four aggregations could be computed in one loop: iterate once, accumulate sum/sumSq/min/max, then derive avg/variance at the end.

issues.summary: executeRelationQueries uses a BiFunction callback abstraction to handle exactly two call sites that differ only in response-map construction
issues.suggestion: Replace the BiFunction parameter with an enum or boolean 'includeDepFields' parameter that controls whether the dep-specific fields (id, description, deprel, total_matches) are included in the map. This keeps the loop unified without the generic callback, and makes the two-case distinction explicit.
evidence: executeRelationQueries signature: 'BiFunction<RelationConfig, ExecutedSketch, Map<String,Object>> builder' parameter.

Called twice: handleFullSketchForType passes a lambda that produces a simple map without 'id', 'description', 'deprel', 'total_matches'; handleDepSketchForType passes the buildRelationResponse static method reference which does include them.

The two call sites differ by about 5 fields in the response map — the abstraction saves roughly 10 lines of loop body across two callers, at the cost of a BiFunction type parameter that interrupts readability.

issues.summary: rotateChunkIfNeeded returns null to signal 'no rotation needed' instead of a typed return value
issues.suggestion: Change rotateChunkIfNeeded to return OptionalLong: return OptionalLong.of(newChunkCount) when rotation occurred, OptionalLong.empty() when not. The call site becomes: OptionalLong rotated = rotateChunkIfNeeded(...); if (rotated.isPresent()) { chunks = rotated.getAsLong(); sentencesInChunk = 0; writer = null; }
evidence: long[] rotated = rotateChunkIfNeeded(writer, outputDir, chunks, sentencesInChunk, sentencesPerChunk); if (rotated != null) { writer = null; chunks = rotated[0]; sentencesInChunk = 0; }

Returning null from a method that normally returns long[] is a null-sentinel pattern: callers must null-check before using the array. A boolean (or an Optional<Long>) would communicate the outcome type-safely.
The long[] return type only ever contains one element (new long[]{newChunkCount}) — wrapping a single long in an array to also encode presence/absence is awkward.

issues.summary: Handler methods assemble response maps via scattered response.put() calls, with only partial deduplication through buildBaseExploreResponse
issues.suggestion: Define a typed response builder or a sealed hierarchy of response types (ExploreResponse, MultiExploreResponse, ComparisonResponse) that encodes which fields are present for each endpoint. ExploreResponseAssembler can be the factory for these types, moving the assembly concern fully to one place. For a lighter approach, ensure buildBaseExploreResponse is used by all three endpoints and the per-endpoint additions are documented in ExploreResponseAssembler.
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: 'response.put("status", "ok"); response.put("relation_type", ...); response.put("parameters", paramsUsed); response.put("seed", result.getSeed())' then calls ExploreResponseAssembler.populateExploreResponse — 4 puts before delegation.
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: similar 4 puts before ExploreResponseAssembler.populateExploreResponse, plus additional 'response.put("seeds", ...) and response.put("seed_count", ...)'.
handleSemanticFieldComparison: builds its own Map without using buildBaseExploreResponse — different response shape with no shared contract.
buildBaseExploreResponse partially deduplicates the common 'status', 'relation_type', 'parameters' fields but callers still add seed-specific fields ad hoc after calling it.

issues.summary: Both explore handlers call relationType().orElseThrow() independently after resolveRelationConfig, repeating validation that could be performed once inside resolveRelationConfig
issues.suggestion: Change resolveRelationConfig to return a record or pair that includes both the validated RelationConfig and the non-null RelationType (since the method already validates its presence). Callers receive the type directly and need not unwrap an Optional that is guaranteed non-empty at this point.
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: 'String relationType = resolvedConfig.relationType().orElseThrow(() -> new IllegalStateException("relationType absent after validation..."))'.
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: identical pattern: 'req.relationConfig().relationType().orElseThrow(() -> new IllegalStateException("relationType absent after validation..."))'.

resolveRelationConfig already validates that relationType is present ('if (relType.isEmpty()) throw new IllegalArgumentException(...)') but returns RelationConfig rather than the extracted RelationType. Callers then have to unwrap the Optional again.

The orElseThrow message 'absent after validation' implies the check is a defensive guard against a post-validation invariant, not a user-visible error — this duplication suggests the seam could be cleaner.

issues.summary: ConcordanceHandlers manually performs two-step pattern substitution (buildFullPattern then substituteAtPosition) instead of using the combined RelationPatternBuilder.buildFullPattern(config, noun, collocate)
issues.suggestion: Replace the two-step call with 'bcqlQuery = RelationPatternBuilder.buildFullPattern(rel.get(), noun, adjective)'. This uses the existing 3-argument overload and removes the direct CqlUtils dependency from ConcordanceHandlers.
evidence: ConcordanceHandlers.handleConcordanceExamples: 'String patternWithHead = RelationPatternBuilder.buildFullPattern(rel.get(), noun); bcqlQuery = CqlUtils.substituteAtPosition(patternWithHead, adjective, rel.get().collocatePosition())'.
RelationPatternBuilder.buildFullPattern(RelationConfig, String headword, String collocateLemma) already encapsulates both substitutions — the 3-argument overload exists precisely for this use case.
The two-step call in ConcordanceHandlers bypasses the intended 3-argument API, making the seam at the api/config boundary inconsistent with the available contract.

issues.summary: CoreCollocate.coverage() Javadoc restates what the method name and one-line body already communicate
issues.suggestion: Remove the Javadoc or replace with a note about an edge case: '/** Returns 0.0 when totalNouns is 0 to avoid division by zero. */' which is the only non-obvious piece of information in the implementation.
evidence: /** Coverage ratio: how many of the discovered nouns share this collocate */
public double coverage() { return totalNouns > 0 ? (double) sharedByCount / totalNouns : 0.0; }
The method name 'coverage' and the record fields 'sharedByCount / totalNouns' in the body already communicate the ratio concept. The Javadoc adds no insight about edge cases, units, or callers.

issues.summary: LogDiceCalculator.relativeFrequency Javadoc restates the formula visible in the one-line implementation
issues.suggestion: Update the Javadoc to: '/** Returns 0.0 when headwordFreq is zero or negative. */' — this documents the only non-obvious behaviour. Remove the formula restatement.
evidence: /** Compute relative frequency (collocate frequency / headword frequency). */
public static double relativeFrequency(long collocateFreq, long headwordFreq) { if (headwordFreq <= 0) { return 0.0; } return (double) collocateFreq / (double) headwordFreq; }
The parenthetical '(collocate frequency / headword frequency)' reproduces what the method body shows directly. The only non-obvious detail is the zero-guard, which is not mentioned in the comment.

issues.summary: getCorsAllowOrigin() private method indirects over a static final constant without adding value
issues.suggestion: Remove getCorsAllowOrigin() and have setCorsHeader() reference CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN directly: exchange.getResponseHeaders().set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN);
evidence: CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN is declared 'private static final String' initialized once at class load.
getCorsAllowOrigin() is a private method that does only 'return CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN'.
setCorsHeader() calls getCorsAllowOrigin() rather than referencing the field directly.
The original intent (dynamic configuration) was eliminated when the field was made static final, but the getter was not removed.

issues.summary: ExploreResponseAssembler belongs in api/ or a new presentation/ package, not model/
issues.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) Create package api/assembly/ or keep in api/ as a package-private class. (2) Move ExploreResponseAssembler.java to src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/api/ExploreResponseAssembler.java and change visibility from public to package-private. (3) Update import in ExplorationHandlers (no change needed if same package). (4) Update ExploreResponseAssemblerTest import path. Validate: mvn test -Dtest=ExploreResponseAssemblerTest. Note: if ExploreResponseAssembler must remain public (e.g., tested externally), consider a dedicated api/assembly sub-package.
evidence: ExploreResponseAssembler.populateExploreResponse and populateComparisonResponse produce Map<String,Object> structures that directly correspond to JSON HTTP response fields (e.g., 'seed_collocates', 'discovered_nouns', 'parameters').
model/package-info declares the package purpose as 'domain model classes for word-sketch query results and semantic-field exploration' — ExploreResponseAssembler is not a domain model.
ExploreResponseAssembler is the only class in model/ that creates HTTP-response-shaped maps; all other model/ files are pure domain value types (records, enums, or value objects).
ExplorationHandlers (in api/) imports and calls ExploreResponseAssembler directly, making api/ depend on model/ for presentation logic.

issues.summary: compareCollocateProfiles declares @NonNull seedNouns but internally null-checks it
issues.suggestion: Remove the null branch: change the condition to 'if (seedNouns.size() < 2)' since @NonNull already promises non-null. If defensive null handling is desired for untrusted callers, remove the @NonNull annotation and add @Nullable, then update callers accordingly.
evidence: Signature: 'public @NonNull ComparisonResult compareCollocateProfiles(@NonNull Set<String> seedNouns, @NonNull ExplorationOptions opts)'
Body line 312: 'if (seedNouns == null || seedNouns.size() < 2)' — null check contradicts @NonNull

issues.summary: Package-private exploreByPattern returns ExplorationResult.empty(seed != null ? seed : "") where the ternary false branch is unreachable
issues.suggestion: Split the guard: 'if (seed == null) return ExplorationResult.empty(""); if (seed.isEmpty()) return ExplorationResult.empty(seed);' — or add @NonNull to the parameter and let callers guarantee non-null, matching the public overload.
evidence: Code: 'if (seed == null || seed.isEmpty()) { return ExplorationResult.empty(seed != null ? seed : ""); }'

The inner ternary 'seed != null ? seed : ""' is inside the branch where seed IS null (outer condition's null case), making the 'seed != null' sub-condition always false when triggered. The true branch ('seed') is unreachable inside this block.
Simplify to: 'return ExplorationResult.empty("");' in the null branch and handle empty separately.

issues.summary: GrammarConfig.getVersion() can return null (per Javadoc) but lacks @Nullable annotation
issues.suggestion: Add @org.jspecify.annotations.Nullable to the return type: 'public @Nullable String getVersion()'
evidence: Javadoc: '@return the grammar version string from the config file (e.g. "1.0"); may be null for legacy configs'
No @Nullable annotation on the return type, inconsistent with the codebase's JSpecify usage pattern.

issues.summary: Redundant null check on @NonNull seedNouns parameter in compareCollocateProfiles
issues.suggestion: Remove the '|| seedNouns == null' sub-expression, leaving only 'if (seedNouns.size() < 2)'.
evidence: 'public @NonNull ComparisonResult compareCollocateProfiles(@NonNull Set<String> seedNouns, ...)' — parameter declared @NonNull
Line 312: 'if (seedNouns == null || seedNouns.size() < 2)' — null condition is always false per @NonNull contract

issues.summary: Dead ternary 'seed != null ? seed : ""' inside a branch reachable only when seed is null
issues.suggestion: Simplify to 'if (seed == null || seed.isEmpty()) { return ExplorationResult.empty(""); }'
evidence: Code: 'if (seed == null || seed.isEmpty()) { return ExplorationResult.empty(seed != null ? seed : ""); }'
When seed IS null: ternary evaluates to "". When seed IS empty string: ternary evaluates to seed (which equals ""). Both paths produce the same result, making the ternary meaningless.

issues.summary: parseSeedSet guards against null seedsParam but callers always pass non-null values
issues.suggestion: Add @NonNull to the parameter and remove the null guard: 'private static Set<String> parseSeedSet(@NonNull String seedsParam) { Set<String> seeds = new LinkedHashSet<>(); for (String s : seedsParam.split(",")) { ... } return seeds; }'
evidence: parseSeedSet(String seedsParam): 'if (seedsParam != null)' — all callers pass a value from requireParam() which returns @NonNull, making the null branch unreachable.
Method signature has no @Nullable annotation on seedsParam.

issues.summary: GrammarConfig.getVersion() can return null but lacks @Nullable annotation
issues.suggestion: Add @org.jspecify.annotations.Nullable to the return: 'public @Nullable String getVersion()'
evidence: Javadoc: 'may be null for legacy configs'
Return type: String (not annotated @Nullable)
Callers cannot statically detect the nullable return.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor.findCollocations() documents null lemma behavior but parameter lacks @Nullable
issues.suggestion: Either (a) add @Nullable to lemma and keep the empty-list-on-null behavior, or (b) add @NonNull and update Javadoc to require non-null lemma, having callers guard before calling.
evidence: Javadoc: '@param lemma The head lemma to search for; returns an empty list silently if null or empty'
Signature: 'List<QueryResults.WordSketchResult> findCollocations(String lemma, ...)' — no @Nullable on lemma

issues.summary: ExploreResponseAssembler public methods have no @NonNull/@Nullable annotations
issues.suggestion: Add @NonNull to all parameters and return types in ExploreResponseAssembler: 'public static void populateExploreResponse(@NonNull Map<String, Object> response, @NonNull ExplorationResult result)'
evidence: All public methods (populateExploreResponse, populateComparisonResponse, formatAdjectiveProfile, serializeEdge, buildEdges, formatSeedCollocates, etc.) have untyped parameters and returns.
Inconsistent with HttpApiUtils in the same codebase which has thorough JSpecify coverage.

issues.summary: getCorsAllowOrigin() private method has no logic — pure pointless indirection over a static final constant
issues.suggestion: Remove getCorsAllowOrigin() and update setCorsHeader() to reference CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN directly: 'exchange.getResponseHeaders().set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN);'
evidence: private static String getCorsAllowOrigin() { return CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN; }
CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN is 'private static final String' initialized once at class load — it is immutable.
setCorsHeader() calls getCorsAllowOrigin() instead of referencing the constant directly.

The only explanation for this pattern is that getCorsAllowOrigin() existed before CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN became a static final (when it was dynamic); the field was made static final but the getter was left behind.

issues.summary: CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN reads System.getProperty at class load time, preventing per-test override
issues.suggestion: If per-test CORS override is needed, convert to a lazily-evaluated supplier or accept it as a constructor parameter to WordSketchApiServer/HttpApiUtils. If not needed, the current approach is acceptable but should be documented as a deliberate constraint. Minimal change: document that CORS origin is fixed at class load and can be overridden only via -Dcors.allow.origin JVM argument at startup.
evidence: private static final String CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN = System.getProperty("cors.allow.origin", "http://localhost:3000");
This is evaluated once when HttpApiUtils is first loaded. Any test that needs a different CORS origin must set the system property before the class is classloaded.
WordSketchApiServerTest starts a server but cannot customize CORS behavior per test.

issues.summary: All 5 live-index test classes are @Disabled, leaving BlackLabQueryExecutor and indexer with zero CI coverage
issues.suggestion: Create a small test corpus (20-50 sentences in CoNLL-U format), index it in a @BeforeAll or @TempDir setup, and run a minimal integration test suite: (1) index the test corpus with BlackLabConllUIndexer, (2) open BlackLabQueryExecutor on the test index, (3) assert findCollocations returns expected collocates, (4) assert executeSurfacePattern returns expected results with correct logDice rank order. This enables CI coverage of the core path without requiring the full 74M-sentence production corpus. Re-enable the @Disabled classes guarded on a CI environment variable or JVM system property.
evidence: All 5 classes are annotated @Disabled.
BlackLabQueryExecutor.findCollocations, executeSurfacePattern, executeDependencyPattern, executeBcqlQuery are the application's primary data methods — none are exercised by any enabled test.
CollocateQueryHelper.buildAndRankCollocates (logDice ranking from real hit groups) is not exercised by enabled tests.
The full index-then-query pipeline is untested in CI.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor exposes two near-identical dependency methods differing only by headPosConstraint parameter
issues.suggestion: Unify into a single method with @Nullable headPosConstraint: 'List<WordSketchResult> executeDependencyPattern(String lemma, String deprel, double minLogDice, int maxResults, @Nullable String headPosConstraint)'. Provide a default interface method for the no-constraint variant: 'default List<WordSketchResult> executeDependencyPattern(String lemma, String deprel, double minLogDice, int maxResults) { return executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, minLogDice, maxResults, null); }'. Update BlackLabQueryExecutor to implement the unified method.
evidence: executeDependencyPattern(String lemma, String deprel, double minLogDice, int maxResults)
executeDependencyPatternWithPos(String lemma, String deprel, double minLogDice, int maxResults, @NonNull String headPosConstraint)
The only difference is the headPosConstraint parameter. Both are in the public QueryExecutor interface, meaning implementations must provide both.
Callers must choose between two methods that do the same thing with/without a pos constraint.

issues.summary: /concordance/examples uses seed/collocate params while sibling /semantic-field/examples uses noun/adjective
issues.suggestion: Align /semantic-field/examples to use seed/collocate: rename ?adjective to ?collocate and ?noun to ?seed in ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExamples(). Update API documentation. The renaming aligns with the codebase's recently-adopted seed/collocate vocabulary (ConcordanceHandlers recently renamed word1/word2 to seed/collocate).
evidence: /api/concordance/examples: ?seed=theory&collocate=good
/api/semantic-field/examples: ?adjective=good&noun=theory
Both endpoints accept a headword + a collocate and return concordance lines; the concepts are the same but the parameter names differ.
A developer using both endpoints must remember which convention each uses.

issues.summary: /api/bcql accepts arbitrary corpus queries with no documented resource limit policy
issues.suggestion: Add a maxResults cap to CorpusQueryHandlers (e.g., MAX_BCQL_RESULTS = 200) and document it in the WordSketchApiServer Javadoc's endpoint listing. Optionally add a request-body size limit mirroring VisualizationHandlers.MAX_REQUEST_BODY_BYTES.
evidence: WordSketchApiServer registers /api/bcql as a POST endpoint with no maxResults or timeout documentation.
The loopback binding prevents external access, but a locally-running browser tab or script could submit an unbounded BCQL query that exhausts corpus search resources.
VisualizationHandlers limits request body to 65536 bytes (MAX_REQUEST_BODY_BYTES) — a similar pattern is absent for /api/bcql.
The security model Javadoc does not mention query resource limits.
